name: Gingerbread Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 fake egg (I forgot to use it though and they were still good)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the pumpkin, brown sugar, molasses, rice milk, and cider vinegar until no lumps of brown sugar remain.
2. Mix in the salt and fake egg.
3. In a separate bowl, combine 3 cups flour with 4 teaspoons baking soda, 2 teaspoons cinnamon, 2 teaspoons ginger, 1/2 teaspoon cloves, 1/2 teaspoon allspice, and 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture until well combined.
5. Add an additional 2 cups of flour and stir just until the dough is absorbed. Avoid over-stirring to prevent lumps.
6. Chill the dough in the refrigerator for several hours before rolling out to 1/4 inch thickness and cutting into cookie shapes.
7. Bake at 350°F until firm (you should be able to leave a print on the cookie with your finger without it breaking).
8. These cookies are delicious warm, but also tasty when chilled. They make great sandwich cookies when paired with a non-fat, non-dairy frozen dessert.
